descEn: Omura Park is a beautiful historical park built on the ruins of Kushima Castle (Omura Castle), which was constructed in 1599 by Omura Yoshiaki, the first lord of the Omura Clan. Designated as one of Japan?s Top 100 Cherry Blossom Spots and one of the Top 100 Historical Parks in Japan, it is highly regarded as one of the best flower viewing locations in Nagasaki Prefecture. In spring, the park is adorned with approximately 2,000 cherry trees, including the rare "Omura-zakura," a designated National Natural Treasure with blossoms containing up to 200 petals. Following the cherry blossoms, from late May to early June, the park transforms into a magnificent garden featuring around 300,000 irises of 100,000 plants, making it one of the largest iris gardens in Kyushu. The scenic combination of historic castle stone walls and vibrant flowers attracts numerous visitors. During the Omura Flower Festival from late March to mid-June, the park is illuminated at night, offering magical views.
